equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: the port skips several specific validation errors, but the generic BlockExecutionError::Validation catch-all still falls through to the fatal Err(err) branch.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Arbitrary BlockExecutionError::Validation values not matched by the specific branches still abort payload building instead of being logged and skipped.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/lib.rs skips InvalidTx, TransactionGasLimitMoreThanAvailableBlockGas, transient async precompile validation, and permanently invalid precompile requests.
The final catch-all branch is Err(err) => return Err(PayloadBuilderError::evm(err)).
Search found no unguarded Err(BlockExecutionError::Validation(error)) branch that logs and continues.

notes: The current code is narrower than the invariant: several validation families are non-fatal, but not the catch-all validation case.
rationale: The catch-all validation branch prevents unknown validation failures from aborting block construction, while the following Err(err) branch still returns a payload builder error for non-validation failures. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: During payload building, any BlockExecutionError::Validation not handled by a more specific branch must be logged and skipped, while non-validation execution errors must still abort payload building.
suggested_falsification_tests: Execute a pool transaction that returns an arbitrary BlockExecutionError::Validation and assert the transaction is skipped with no PayloadBuilderError; execute one returning a non-validation BlockExecutionError and assert payload building returns an EVM error.
summary: Generic validation errors still abort payload building.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Inject an unrecognized BlockExecutionError::Validation from transaction execution and assert payload building returns an EVM error rather than skipping the transaction.
title: Builder skips validation errors
